opportunities.ai_thesis: APP has pulled back 38% from its 52-week high, creating a technically attractive entry into one of the highest-conviction names in the ad-tech space, with a composite score of 109/100 signaling broad-based strength in fundamentals and momentum. Retail sentiment remains firmly bullish at 80% on StockTwits, suggesting the market views this dip as a buying opportunity rather than a fundamental breakdown. With no insider selling pressure and the stock well off peak levels, the risk/reward favors initiating or adding to positions now ahead of any re-acceleration in mobile advertising spend.

KKR presents a compelling entry at $87.13 — 43% below its 52-week high — as the firm advances a potential $3B+ sale of CoolIT, a near-term value realization catalyst that could meaningfully surface NAV while JPMorgan's pullback from private credit lending reduces competitive pressure on KKR's core business. Institutional accumulation by Winton Group and 13 insider buys over the past 90 days signal smart money conviction at current levels, with retail sentiment confirming the setup at 75% bullish on StockTwits. The confluence of a depressed price, an identifiable monetization event, and reduced sector competition makes this an asymmetric risk/reward entry before the CoolIT transaction closes.

The Trade Desk's newly announced partnership with OpenAI positions it as the infrastructure layer for AI-driven programmatic advertising, a structural shift that should re-rate the stock well above its current 70% drawdown from highs. With 4 insider buys in the last 90 days and StockTwits sentiment at 93% bullish, institutional and retail conviction is building simultaneously at a $13B market cap that prices in little of the OpenAI ecosystem optionality. At $27.28, the risk/reward is asymmetric — buy now before the partnership details catalyze a multiple expansion back toward historical norms.

POET Technologies presents a compelling entry at $7.44 (21% below 52-week high) following its OFC 2026 unveiling of Blazar and Starlight hybrid lasers — purpose-built for AI data center interconnects at a time when hyperscalers are under intense pressure to reduce optical networking costs. With a 62-day runway to full catalyst realization, the current price offers asymmetric upside before Wall Street fully prices in the commercial momentum from these next-generation products. Overwhelmingly bullish retail sentiment (+0.80, 100% StockTwits bullish) combined with a $1.1B market cap still well off its highs creates an attractive risk/reward setup before institutional attention catches up.

BofA's price target raise to $57 signals institutional conviction in Alamos Gold's near-term re-rating, offering an 15% upside from current levels with the analyst upgrade acting as an immediate catalyst. At $49.42 — 11% below its 52-week high — AGI presents a technically attractive entry at a pullback within an intact uptrend, supported by 100% bullish retail sentiment on StockTwits reinforcing the momentum setup. With a $20.8B market cap providing liquidity and the BUY score of 126 well above threshold, this is a defined-risk trade targeting the $57 analyst price target with the upgrade narrative as the near-term driver.

OXY presents a compelling entry at $55.58 as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East (Iran conflict, Strait of Hormuz) create near-term oil price volatility that historically resolves higher for integrated producers with Occidental's domestic production profile. The company's proactive balance sheet management — expanding its debt tender to $1.2B with covenant changes settled March 9 — signals management confidence and reduces refinancing risk precisely when investors need it most. With insiders buying, StockTwits sentiment at 100% bullish, and the stock within 1% of its 52-week high (a breakout setup rather than a warning), the risk/reward favors initiating a position now before geopolitical premium fully reprices into the stock.

CoreWeave's partnership with Perplexity on Nvidia GB200 systems, set to materialize over the next 65 days, validates its positioning as the premier high-performance AI cloud infrastructure provider — with $5.1B in 2025 revenue (+168% YoY) and 57% adjusted EBITDA margins proving the business model at scale. At $81.96, CRWV trades 56% below its 52-week high with 100% bullish retail sentiment and a score of 109/100, offering an asymmetric entry into a hypergrowth name that the market has repriced without fundamental deterioration. The flexible AI cloud capacity launch further broadens the revenue base beyond hyperscaler dependency, making this pullback a structurally sound accumulation point ahead of the catalyst window.

Zebra Technologies' launch of its 'Orchestrated Care' initiative positions the company to capture significant share in the $50B+ hospital efficiency market, with the catalyst expected to drive revenue visibility within the next 62 days. At $213.67 — 39% below its 52-week high — ZBRA offers a compelling mean-reversion entry as depressed sentiment (18% bullish on StockTwits) and negative news flow historically precede institutional accumulation, supported by recent insider buying. The combination of a concrete product catalyst, beaten-down valuation, and Zebra's proven enterprise sales motion in healthcare scanning infrastructure makes this an asymmetric setup ahead of the initiative gaining traction.

Gen Digital's landmark victory in the Columbia University patent lawsuit eliminates a $481M liability overhang that had been suppressing the stock, and the market has not yet fully repriced this risk removal with shares still trading 34% below their 52-week high. Despite negative insider activity, retail sentiment is decisively bullish at 88% on StockTwits with a +0.43 news sentiment score, suggesting institutional catch-up buying could drive near-term momentum as the legal resolution becomes fully digested. At $21.24 with a $13.1B market cap, the asymmetric risk/reward favors entry now — the primary bear case (lawsuit liability) is resolved, creating a clear re-rating catalyst without a defined expiration window.

Arcutis Biotherapeutics (ARQT) offers a compelling risk/reward setup with a regulatory approval catalyst expected within ~110 days, as Phase 3 open-label extension data for ZORYVE cream 0.05% in pediatric atopic dermatitis (ages 2-5) positions the company for a label expansion that could meaningfully broaden its addressable market. The stock sits 25% below its 52-week high despite the company achieving profitability and raising 2026 guidance — a technical reset that creates an attractive entry before the catalyst window while retail sentiment is already 100% bullish on StockTwits, suggesting momentum will build. Notably absent insider buying keeps conviction measured, but the combination of strong fundamentals, a de-risked pipeline with positive safety/efficacy readouts already in hand, and a $3B market cap that has yet to reprice for the expanded pediatric indication makes this a timely, high-conviction setup ahead of approval.

IREN's planned acquisition of 50,000 additional GPUs positions the company to hit its $3.7B AI Cloud ARR target by end-2026, a catalyst now 62 days out that the market has not yet priced in. At $41.98 — 45% below its 52-week high — the stock offers a technically distressed entry on a fundamentally expanding business, with retail sentiment (94% bullish on StockTwits) likely to amplify upside as the expansion timeline tightens. Neutral insider activity removes a key overhang risk, and a score of 84/100 clears the BUY threshold with meaningful margin, making this an asymmetric setup before the GPU buildout newsflow accelerates.

Adobe's expanded MLB partnership signals accelerating enterprise adoption of its AI-driven creative tools, positioning ADBE to capture recurring revenue from live sports media — a high-visibility, high-margin vertical. At $273.70, the stock trades 38% below its 52-week high with overwhelmingly bullish retail sentiment (+0.59, 100% StockTwits bulls), offering a technically oversold entry with asymmetric upside as the partnership generates near-term press and platform visibility. Despite negative insider activity warranting a stop-loss discipline, the 90/100 score and sentiment setup favor initiating a position now ahead of partnership-driven earnings commentary.

ADMA Biologics presents a compelling entry at $15.67 — 39% below its 52-week high — as multiple directors have put real capital to work with 7,000+ shares purchased (~$110k-$157k), signaling conviction ahead of Q1 2026 earnings expected in May. The 100% bullish StockTwits sentiment combined with insider accumulation at depressed prices suggests the market has overcorrected, creating an asymmetric setup before the earnings catalyst can close the gap to prior highs. With a $3.7B market cap and strong management-aligned buying, the risk/reward favors initiating a position now with the May earnings report as the near-term inflection point.

BofA Securities' fresh Buy initiation with a $244 price target signals institutional conviction that LGND's royalty-driven model is underappreciated, offering ~10% upside from current levels within a 33-day catalyst window. At $221.24 — just 3% off its 52-week high — price action confirms accumulation ahead of the coverage launch, while 100% bullish StockTwits sentiment suggests retail momentum is building behind the institutional thesis. Despite negative insider activity, the asymmetric setup favors entry now: a well-capitalized analyst initiation typically attracts follow-on coverage and fund flows, making this a high-conviction swing ahead of the catalyst.

events.summary: Ligand Pharmaceuticals has a PDUFA decision date for FILSPARI (sNDA) for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis treatment.
Ligand Pharmaceuticals' sNDA for FILSPARI in focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS) has PDUFA date of January 13, 2026.

opportunities.ai_thesis: Limited bull case evident. Stock has declined 45% from highs without catalysts, news support, or insider buying conviction. Represents a fallen angel awaiting operational improvements or market re-rating, not a compelling opportunity at present.

BRCTF presents a compelling risk/reward setup with an FDA catalyst expected within 74 days, while the stock trades 13% below its 52-week high — offering a technically attractive entry before the event-driven re-rating. Retail sentiment is uniformly bullish (100% StockTwits) with a +0.40 news score, suggesting momentum is building without yet being fully priced in at the $2.9B market cap level. With neutral insider activity avoiding any distribution signal and the score clearing the 85/100 BUY threshold, initiating a position now captures the pre-catalyst setup before broader market attention arrives.

Workday's post-earnings selloff — driven by guidance cuts, a CEO transition, and analyst downgrades — has pushed shares 50% below their 52-week high to $137.84, creating a mean-reversion setup as the market has likely over-discounted near-term execution risk in a best-in-class HCM platform with deep enterprise switching costs. With a score of 97/100 and 80% retail bullish sentiment against a washed-out price near revised fair value of $184, the risk/reward asymmetry is compelling ahead of the next earnings catalyst in ~70 days. Enter here with a target recovery toward $184 (revised fair value), accepting that negative insider activity and reduced long-term targets cap near-term upside until the new CEO articulates a credible strategic reset.

TVTX offers a compelling risk/reward setup ahead of the anticipated FDA approval of FILSPARI for FSGS around mid-April 2026 — a label expansion that meaningfully enlarges the addressable market beyond its current indication. At $28.47, the stock sits 32% below its 52-week high with muted retail sentiment (57% bullish on StockTwits) and zero insider buying, conditions that historically precede re-rating events as institutional money positions ahead of binary catalysts rather than after. With a score of 101 and 33 days to the catalyst, the current entry captures asymmetric upside before approval-driven momentum compresses the discount.

events.summary: Travere Therapeutics' FILSPARI (sparsentan) sNDA accepted for Focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS).
Travere Therapeutics' FILSPARI (sparsentan) sNDA accepted for Focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS).
Travere Therapeutics has an sNDA accepted for FILSPARI (sparsentan) for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS).
Travere Therapeutics has a PDUFA decision date for FILSPARI (sNDA) for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis treatment.
Travere Therapeutics has a PDUFA date for sNDA of FILSPARI for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis on January 13, 2026.

opportunities.ai_thesis: The stock's 22% decline from recent highs presents a potential value opportunity for contrarian investors. Neutral market sentiment and lack of panic suggest the decline may be cyclical rather than fundamental deterioration.

events.summary: Banc of California filed a new Form S-3 shelf and prospectus supplement on March 6, 2026 to permit resale of voting common stock by selling stockholders.
Banc of California filed a new Form S-3 shelf and prospectus supplement on March 6, 2026 to permit resale of voting common stock by selling stockholders.

opportunities.ai_thesis: Elliott Investment Management's $1 billion convertible note position in Pinterest, paired with a new $3.5 billion buyback program, signals a high-conviction activist catalyst that historically precedes meaningful operational and capital allocation improvements. At $19.38 — 51% below its 52-week high — PINS offers a compressed entry with asymmetric upside as Elliott's influence is likely to surface value through cost discipline, strategic focus, or a potential sale process. With StockTwits sentiment at 82% bullish and the stock scoring 83/100, momentum is building ahead of any formal Elliott agenda disclosure, making this an actionable entry before the market fully prices in activist-driven change.

Cheniere Energy (LNG) presents a compelling buy following its earnings beat, with Argus reiterating its buy rating and a surge in options activity signaling institutional anticipation of continued upside. Trading just 3% below its 52-week high, LNG is consolidating near breakout territory amid elevated oil prices driven by Middle East tensions and strong sector momentum—a technical setup that historically precedes continuation moves. Enter now before a potential catalyst-driven push to new highs, while maintaining awareness that negative insider activity warrants a disciplined stop-loss below recent support.
Biotech stock down 25% with pending FDA catalyst offers potential asymmetric play IF approval materializes. However, insider selling significantly undermines confidence in positive outcome.

If the undefined FDA catalyst delivers positive results in 111 days, a 17% down stock could experience mean reversion. However, the lack of defined details or market attention makes this a speculative bet on an unconfirmed event.
